recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Cauliflower Cheese Soup With Buttered Sourdough Croutons

Creamy cauliflower cheese soup blended with cheddar, hard cheese and milk, finished with olive oil, black pepper and dried thyme.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 40 minutes

Yield: 3 soup bowls

Meal role: cheap family dinner

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Cowbelle Whole Milk 3.6% Fat - 500ml
- Emporium British Mature Cheddar Cheese - 150g
- Emporium Grated Hard Cheese - 80g
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 20g

### Fruit/Veg

- Nature's Pick British Cauliflower - 1 whole cauliflower
- Nature's Pick Brown Onions - 1 medium onion
- Nature's Pick Garlic - 3 small cloves

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 2 slices, about 120g total
- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 1 1/2 tbsp (22.5ml) total: 3/4 tbsp (11.25ml) for the onion base, plus 2 1/4 tsp (11.25ml) for finishing
- Ready, Set... Cook! Dried Thyme - 2 tsp (2g) total: 1 tsp (1g) in the cauliflower bowl, plus 1 tsp (1g) for finishing
- Morrisons Dijon Mustard - 5g
- Solesta White Wine Vinegar - 5ml

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 10 inch cast iron frying pan
- Nutribullet Smart Touch Blender
- Ladle
- Chopping board
- Knife
- Grater
- Spatula or wooden spoon

## Mise En Place

- Onion and garlic bowl: 1 medium onion chopped with 3 small garlic cloves sliced. Used in Step 1.
- Cauliflower bowl: 1 whole cauliflower cut into florets and small stalk pieces, with 1 tsp (1g) dried thyme. Used in Step 2.
- Cheese bowl: 150g grated cheddar, 80g hard cheese, 5g Dijon and 5ml white wine vinegar. Used in Step 4.
- Crouton bowl: 2 slices white sourdough diced with 20g butter ready by the cast iron pan. Used in Step 3.
- Serving setup: 3 soup bowls, 2 1/4 tsp (11.25ml) olive oil for finishing, 1 tsp (1g) dried thyme for finishing and the black pepper grinder. Used in Step 6.

## Method

### 1. Soften the onion and garlic

Heat the olive oil in the saute pan over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic with a pinch of salt. Cook for about 6-7 minutes until soft but not browned.

Uses:

- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 3/4 tbsp (11.25ml)
- Onion and garlic bowl - all

### 2. Simmer the cauliflower

Add the cauliflower, milk, water and dried thyme. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover loosely and cook for about 18-20 minutes until the cauliflower stalk pieces are soft.

Uses:

- Cauliflower bowl - all
- Cowbelle Whole Milk 3.6% Fat - 500ml
- Water - enough to help the liquid cover the cauliflower, about 200ml

### 3. Make the buttered croutons

While the soup simmers, melt the butter in the cast iron pan. Add the diced sourdough and cook for about 5-6 minutes, tossing until golden and crisp.

Uses:

- Crouton bowl - all: 2 diced sourdough slices and 20g butter

### 4. Add cheese and blend smooth

Take the soup off the heat. Stir in the cheese bowl while the soup is still hot so the cheese softens into the cauliflower base. Blend smooth in the Nutribullet jug for about 1-2 minutes, then return the soup to the saute pan.

Uses:

- Simmered cauliflower, milk, onion and garlic soup base - all
- Cheese bowl - all

### 5. Warm and season the soup

Warm the blended soup gently on low heat for about 2-3 minutes until glossy and hot enough to serve. Do not boil hard after the dairy goes in, because hard boiling can make cheese split or turn grainy. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.

Uses:

- Blended cauliflower cheese soup - all

### 6. Serve the dinner bowls

Ladle the finished soup into 3 bowls. Add croutons, then finish each bowl with a measured olive oil circle, black pepper and dried thyme.

Uses:

- Cauliflower cheese soup - all
- Buttered sourdough croutons - all
- Serving setup - 3 dinner bowls, olive oil finish: Luke 1 tsp (5ml), Sarah 3/4 tsp (3.75ml), Louis 1/2 tsp (2.5ml); dried thyme finish: 1 tsp (1g) scattered across the bowls to match bowl sizes; black pepper

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Cauliflower Cheese Soup | 45% of the finished soup batch | 33% of the finished soup batch | 22% of the finished soup batch |
| Buttered Sourdough Croutons | 40% of the croutons | 35% of the croutons | 25% of the croutons |
| Olive Oil Thyme Finish | 1 tsp (5ml) olive oil + the largest thyme scatter | 3/4 tsp (3.75ml) olive oil + a medium thyme scatter | 1/2 tsp (2.5ml) olive oil + a small thyme scatter |

## Nutrition And Cost

| Person | Kcal | Protein | Carbs | Fat | Cost | Fullness |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Luke | 941.3 | 49.6g | 50.6g | 61.6g | GBP 2.09 | 120.2 |
| Sarah | 714.6 | 37.0g | 40.0g | 46.1g | GBP 1.57 | 91.1 |
| Louis | 483.4 | 24.8g | 27.7g | 31.1g | GBP 1.07 | 61.6 |

## Cook's Notes

- The cheeses go into the hot soup before blending. This matches Luke's proven method and gives the smoothest texture.
- Blend the source-style soup volume once, then return it to the saute pan to warm gently.
- Keep the heat gentle after blending so the dairy stays smooth.
- Salt, black pepper and water are method helpers, so they are not counted in the shopping or nutrition rows.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Creamy Garlic Chicken And Broccoli

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 38 minutes

Meal role: dinner

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Chicken breast fillets - 490g

### Carbs

- British white potatoes - 510g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick British Broccoli - 295g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Brown onions - 110g
- Lighter soft cheese - 60g
- Parmesan cheese - 25g
- Garlic - 12g
- Olive oil - 15ml
- Unsalted butter - 10g
- Smoked paprika - 2g
- Dried oregano - 1.5g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- mixing bowl
- tongs
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Potato pan: Peel if wanted, then cut the potatoes into even chunky pieces so they boil and steam dry at the same pace.
- Broccoli bowl: Cut the fresh broccoli into bite-size florets and weigh 295g into the bowl so it is ready to cook after the potatoes.
- Chicken board: Slice the chicken into bite-size pieces so it browns quickly in one wide pan and stays easy to coat with the finished sauce.
- Sauce bowl: Keep the sliced onion, crushed garlic, cream cheese, parmesan, butter and measured dried seasonings together so the creamy sauce can be built in one smooth pan sequence.
- Serve setup: Set out the colander for the potatoes, 3 dinner plates, the probe thermometer and a clean plate for the cooked chicken before the hot steps begin.

## Method

### 1. Boil the potatoes

Cover the potato pan with cold water, bring it to the boil, add salt, then simmer for about 15-18 minutes until the chunks are tender. Drain them in the colander and let them steam dry for about 2 minutes.

Uses:

- Boiled potatoes - all: British white potatoes 510g

### 2. Cook the broccoli

Refill the potato saucepan with fresh boiling water. Tip in the Broccoli bowl and simmer for about 3-4 minutes until bright green and just tender, then drain it well and keep it warm.

Uses:

- Fresh broccoli - all: Nature's Pick British Broccoli 295g

### 3. Brown the chicken

Heat the saute pan over medium-high heat with a little of the measured oil. Add the Chicken board pieces in one pan load and cook for about 7-9 minutes, turning as needed, until the thickest piece reaches 75C. Move the chicken to the clean resting plate from the Serve setup.

Uses:

- Creamy garlic chicken pieces - all: Chicken breast fillets 490g

### 4. Build the creamy garlic sauce

Lower the heat to medium. Add the Sauce bowl onion with the remaining measured oil and butter, then cook for about 6 minutes until soft. Stir in the Sauce bowl garlic, paprika and oregano for about 30 seconds. Add a splash of water, then stir in the Sauce bowl cream cheese and parmesan for about 2-3 minutes until smooth and glossy.

Uses:

- Creamy garlic onion sauce - all: Brown onions 110g, Lighter soft cheese 60g, Parmesan cheese 25g, Garlic 12g, Olive oil 15ml, Unsalted butter 10g, Smoked paprika 2g, Dried oregano 1.5g

### 5. Finish the creamy chicken and plate

Return the chicken to the pan and coat it in the sauce for about 1-2 minutes until everything is hot. Plate the creamy garlic chicken, drained potatoes and drained broccoli by the saved finished-batch shares.

Uses:

- Finished serving elements - as shown in the Plate table

## Plate

| Boiled Potatoes | about 47% of the drained potato pan | about 31% of the drained potato pan | about 22% of the drained potato pan |
| Fresh Broccoli | about 46% of the drained broccoli | about 27% of the drained broccoli | about 27% of the drained broccoli |

- Sarah: 457.3 kcal, 48.5g protein, 39.3g carbs, 12.9g fat, GBP 1.80

# Buttered Poached Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Soft poached eggs on hot buttered white sourdough, seasoned simply at the plate.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 25 minutes

Yield: 3 plates

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 8 eggs
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 15g total

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 3 slices, about 180g total

## Equipment

- 20 cm saucepan with lid
- Slotted spoon
- Toaster
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: 3 slices of white sourdough with 15g unsalted butter ready beside them. Used in Step 2.
- Egg cups: 8 eggs, cracked into cups if you want cleaner poaching, or kept whole if cracking straight into the water. Used in Step 3.
- Finish station: salt and black pepper beside the plates. Used in Step 4.

## Method

### 1. Heat the poaching water

Bring the saucepan of water to a gentle simmer, then lower the heat so the water is moving but not bubbling hard. This usually takes about 5 minutes.

Uses:

- Water - enough for poaching

### 2. Toast and butter the sourdough

Toast the sourdough until golden and firm. Butter each slice while it is hot so the butter melts into the toast. This should take about 4 minutes.

Uses:

- Toast board - all: 3 slices white sourdough and 15g unsalted butter

### 3. Poach the eggs

Slide the eggs into the water in controlled rounds if the pan is crowded. Cook gently until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft. Allow about 3-4 minutes per round.

Uses:

- Egg cups - all: 8 eggs

### 4. Plate and season

Lift the eggs out with a slotted spoon and drain briefly. Plate using the table below, then finish each plate with salt and black pepper.

Uses:

- Buttered sourdough toast - all
- Poached eggs - all
- Finish station - salt and black pepper to taste

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Buttered sourdough toast | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice |
| Poached eggs | 3 eggs | 3 eggs | 2 eggs |

## Nutrition And Cost

| Person | Kcal | Protein | Carbs | Fat | Cost | Fullness |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Luke | 377.2 | 24.4g | 28.3g | 19.3g | GBP 0.86 | 95.1 |
| Sarah | 377.2 | 24.4g | 28.3g | 19.3g | GBP 0.86 | 95.1 |
| Louis | 310.1 | 17.9g | 28.3g | 14.7g | GBP 0.67 | 74.6 |

## Cook's Notes

- Butter the toast as soon as it comes out, then put the eggs on top.
- If the pan feels crowded, poach the eggs in two or three controlled rounds.
- Plate Sarah and Louis first if useful, then finish Luke's 3-egg plate.
- Salt and black pepper are neutral helpers, so they are mentioned in the method but not counted in the shopping or nutrition rows.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Buttery Scrambled Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 18 minutes

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Carbs

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 180g

### Protein

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 8 each

### Dairy

- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 23g

### Finish


## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- jug
- fork or small whisk
- silicone spatula
- toaster
- plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: Set out 3 slices of white sourdough and keep 15g of the butter ready for the toast.
- Egg jug: Crack 8 eggs into a jug, add a light pinch of salt and a light grind of black pepper per person, then whisk until the eggs are mostly combined.
- Pan butter dish: Keep 8g butter ready by the warmed saute pan so it can melt just before the egg jug goes in.

## Method

### 1. Warm the saute pan

Put the 32 cm tri-ply saute pan on the lowest heat for about 2-3 minutes so the base warms gently without browning the butter later.

### 2. Whisk the eggs

Crack the 8 eggs into the Egg jug, add the measured salt and black pepper, then whisk for about 1 minute until the yolks and whites are mostly combined.

Uses:

- Soft scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 8 each

### 3. Toast and butter the bread

Use the Toast board: put the 3 sourdough slices in the toaster. When they are done, butter each hot slice with 5g of the toast butter. This should take about 4 minutes.

Uses:

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - amount stated in this step: Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf 180g
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 23g

### 4. Cook the eggs softly

Use the Pan butter dish: melt 8g butter in the warm pan, pour in the Egg jug, then slowly scrape the bottom up with the spatula for about 4-5 minutes until the eggs are almost cooked but still soft.

Uses:

- Soft scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 8 each
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 23g

### 5. Finish off the heat

Take the pan off the heat while the eggs are still slightly glossy. Finish the toast, then spoon the scrambled eggs over the buttered slices by finished serving element: Luke and Sarah get about two fifths each, and Louis gets about one quarter. This should take about 1 minute.

Uses:

- Soft scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 8 each
- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - amount stated in this step: Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf 180g
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 23g

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Buttered Sourdough Toast | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice |
| Soft Scrambled Eggs | about two fifths of the cooked egg batch | about two fifths of the cooked egg batch | about one quarter of the cooked egg batch |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 413.8 kcal, 25.8g protein, 28.2g carbs, 22.9g fat, GBP 0.87
- Sarah: 413.8 kcal, 25.8g protein, 28.2g carbs, 22.9g fat, GBP 0.87
- Louis: 334.3 kcal, 18.9g protein, 28.2g carbs, 17.1g fat, GBP 0.68
